heading: Other protections for users of commercial electronic mail
label: 7704 Other protections for users of commercial electronic mail

children.heading: Requirements for transmission of messages
children.label: a Requirements for transmission of messages

children.heading: Prohibition of false or misleading transmission information
children.label: 1 Prohibition of false or misleading transmission information
A
children.text: header information that is technically accurate but includes an originating electronic mail address, domain name, or Internet Protocol address the access to which for purposes of initiating the message was obtained by means of false or fraudulent pretenses or representations shall be considered materially misleading;

children.label: B
children.text: a “from” line (the line identifying or purporting to identify a person initiating the message) that accurately identifies any person who initiated the message shall not be considered materially false or materially misleading; and

children.label: C
children.text: header information shall be considered materially misleading if it fails to identify accurately a protected computer used to initiate the message because the person initiating the message knowingly uses another protected computer to relay or retransmit the message for purposes of disguising its origin.

children.heading: Prohibition of deceptive subject headings
children.label: 2 Prohibition of deceptive subject headings
children.text: It is unlawful for any person to initiate the transmission to a protected computer of a commercial electronic mail message if such person has actual knowledge, or knowledge fairly implied on the basis of objective circumstances, that a subject heading of the message would be likely to mislead a recipient, acting reasonably under the circumstances, about a material fact regarding the contents or subject matter of the message (consistent with the criteria used in enforcement of ).section 45 of this title

children.heading: Inclusion of return address or comparable mechanism in commercial electronic mail
children.label: 3 Inclusion of return address or comparable mechanism in commercial electronic mail
i
children.text: a recipient may use to submit, in a manner specified in the message, a reply electronic mail message or other form of Internet-based communication requesting not to receive future commercial electronic mail messages from that sender at the electronic mail address where the message was received; and

children.label: ii
children.text: remains capable of receiving such messages or communications for no less than 30 days after the transmission of the original message.

children.heading: More detailed options possible
children.label: B More detailed options possible
children.text: The person initiating a commercial electronic mail message may comply with subparagraph (A)(i) by providing the recipient a list or menu from which the recipient may choose the specific types of commercial electronic mail messages the recipient wants to receive or does not want to receive from the sender, if the list or menu includes an option under which the recipient may choose not to receive any commercial electronic mail messages from the sender.

children.heading: Temporary inability to receive messages or process requests
children.label: C Temporary inability to receive messages or process requests
children.text: A return electronic mail address or other mechanism does not fail to satisfy the requirements of subparagraph (A) if it is unexpectedly and temporarily unable to receive messages or process requests due to a technical problem beyond the control of the sender if the problem is corrected within a reasonable time period.

children.heading: Prohibition of transmission of commercial electronic mail after objection
children.label: 4 Prohibition of transmission of commercial electronic mail after objection
i
children.text: for the sender to initiate the transmission to the recipient, more than 10 business days after the receipt of such request, of a commercial electronic mail message that falls within the scope of the request;

children.label: ii
children.text: for any person acting on behalf of the sender to initiate the transmission to the recipient, more than 10 business days after the receipt of such request, of a commercial electronic mail message with actual knowledge, or knowledge fairly implied on the basis of objective circumstances, that such message falls within the scope of the request;

children.label: iii
children.text: for any person acting on behalf of the sender to assist in initiating the transmission to the recipient, through the provision or selection of addresses to which the message will be sent, of a commercial electronic mail message with actual knowledge, or knowledge fairly implied on the basis of objective circumstances, that such message would violate clause (i) or (ii); or

children.label: iv
children.text: for the sender, or any other person who knows that the recipient has made such a request, to sell, lease, exchange, or otherwise transfer or release the electronic mail address of the recipient (including through any transaction or other transfer involving mailing lists bearing the electronic mail address of the recipient) for any purpose other than compliance with this chapter or other provision of law.

children.heading: Subsequent affirmative consent
children.label: B Subsequent affirmative consent
children.text: A prohibition in subparagraph (A) does not apply if there is affirmative consent by the recipient subsequent to the request under subparagraph (A).

children.heading: Inclusion of identifier, opt-out, and physical address in commercial electronic mail
children.label: 5 Inclusion of identifier, opt-out, and physical address in commercial electronic mail
i
children.text: clear and conspicuous identification that the message is an advertisement or solicitation;

children.label: ii
children.text: clear and conspicuous notice of the opportunity under paragraph (3) to decline to receive further commercial electronic mail messages from the sender; and

children.label: iii
children.text: a valid physical postal address of the sender.

children.label: B
children.text: Subparagraph (A)(i) does not apply to the transmission of a commercial electronic mail message if the recipient has given prior affirmative consent to receipt of the message.

children.heading: Materially
children.label: 6 Materially
children.text: For purposes of paragraph (1), the term “materially”, when used with respect to false or misleading header information, includes the alteration or concealment of header information in a manner that would impair the ability of an Internet access service processing the message on behalf of a recipient, a person alleging a violation of this section, or a law enforcement agency to identify, locate, or respond to a person who initiated the electronic mail message or to investigate the alleged violation, or the ability of a recipient of the message to respond to a person who initiated the electronic message.

children.heading: Aggravated violations relating to commercial electronic mail
children.label: b Aggravated violations relating to commercial electronic mail
i
children.text: the electronic mail address of the recipient was obtained using an automated means from an Internet website or proprietary online service operated by another person, and such website or online service included, at the time the address was obtained, a notice stating that the operator of such website or online service will not give, sell, or otherwise transfer addresses maintained by such website or online service to any other party for the purposes of initiating, or enabling others to initiate, electronic mail messages; or

children.label: ii
children.text: the electronic mail address of the recipient was obtained using an automated means that generates possible electronic mail addresses by combining names, letters, or numbers into numerous permutations.

children.heading: Disclaimer
children.label: B Disclaimer
children.text: Nothing in this paragraph creates an ownership or proprietary interest in such electronic mail addresses.

children.heading: Automated creation of multiple electronic mail accounts
children.label: 2 Automated creation of multiple electronic mail accounts
children.text: It is unlawful for any person to use scripts or other automated means to register for multiple electronic mail accounts or online user accounts from which to transmit to a protected computer, or enable another person to transmit to a protected computer, a commercial electronic mail message that is unlawful under subsection (a).

children.heading: Relay or retransmission through unauthorized access
children.label: 3 Relay or retransmission through unauthorized access
children.text: It is unlawful for any person knowingly to relay or retransmit a commercial electronic mail message that is unlawful under subsection (a) from a protected computer or computer network that such person has accessed without authorization.

children.label: A
children.text: the purposes of subsection (a);

children.label: B
children.text: the interests of recipients of commercial electronic mail; and

children.label: C
children.text: the burdens imposed on senders of lawful commercial electronic mail; and

specify additional activities or practices to which subsection (b) applies if the Commission determines that those activities or practices are contributing substantially to the proliferation of commercial electronic mail messages that are unlawful under subsection (a).

children.heading: Requirement to place warning labels on commercial electronic mail containing sexually oriented material
children.label: d Requirement to place warning labels on commercial electronic mail containing sexually oriented material
A
children.text: fail to include in subject heading for the electronic mail message the marks or notices prescribed by the Commission under this subsection; or

children.label: i
children.text: to the extent required or authorized pursuant to paragraph (2), any such marks or notices;

children.label: ii
children.text: the information required to be included in the message pursuant to subsection (a)(5); and

children.label: iii
children.text: instructions on how to access, or a mechanism to access, the sexually oriented material.

children.heading: Prior affirmative consent
children.label: 2 Prior affirmative consent
children.text: Paragraph (1) does not apply to the transmission of an electronic mail message if the recipient has given prior affirmative consent to receipt of the message.

children.heading: Prescription of marks and notices
children.label: 3 Prescription of marks and notices
children.text: Not later than 120 days after , the Commission in consultation with the Attorney General shall prescribe clearly identifiable marks or notices to be included in or associated with commercial electronic mail that contains sexually oriented material, in order to inform the recipient of that fact and to facilitate filtering of such electronic mail. The Commission shall publish in the Federal Register and provide notice to the public of the marks or notices prescribed under this paragraph.December 16, 2003

children.heading: Definition
children.label: 4 Definition
children.text: In this subsection, the term “sexually oriented material” means any material that depicts sexually explicit conduct (as that term is defined in ), unless the depiction constitutes a small and insignificant part of the whole, the remainder of which is not primarily devoted to sexual matters.section 2256 of title 18

children.heading: Penalty
children.label: 5 Penalty
children.text: Whoever knowingly violates paragraph (1) shall be fined under title 18, or imprisoned not more than 5 years, or both.
